description: Add faster-whisper as secondary ASR model running alongside FireRedASR. When gemini-merge post-processor is active, both models transcribe the same audio, and Gemini merges the results — keeping Chinese from FireRedASR and English terms from faster-whisper. Fallback to single-ASR gemini-fix if secondary model fails.

userStories.title: Add faster-whisper framework to ModelLoader and ModelInference
userStories.description: Add 'faster-whisper' framework support in model_configs.py. ModelLoader loads WhisperModel with CPU-only, large-v3-turbo, int8 compute. ModelInference transcribes by joining segments. Language auto-detected (not forced) to handle mixed Chinese-English.
acceptanceCriteria: ModelLoader.load_faster_whisper_model() creates WhisperModel(model_size, device='cpu', compute_type='int8')
ModelLoader.load_model() dispatches framework='faster-whisper' to load_faster_whisper_model()
ModelInference.transcribe_faster_whisper(model, audio_path) returns joined text from model.transcribe() segments
Language parameter NOT forced — auto-detection handles mixed Chinese-English
Model downloads automatically on first use via HuggingFace hub (cached in ~/.cache/huggingface/)
Error handling: import failure or model load failure raises clear error with install instructions
CRITIC-R10-L2: model.transcribe() returns Tuple[Iterable[Segment], TranscriptionInfo] — must unpack: segments, info = model.transcribe(audio_path), iterate segments only
CRITIC-R10-L3: faster-whisper DOES support hotwords param — plan should not claim otherwise. Hotwords not used for secondary ASR but available for standalone mode
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add faster-whisper standalone model preset

force_cpu=True in preset (Whisper must not compete with FireRedASR for VRAM)
config includes model_size='large-v3-turbo', compute_type='int8'
Preset has descriptive name and description mentioning English strength and CPU-only
Existing presets unchanged
Tests pass

userStories.title: Load faster-whisper as secondary ASR in ASRDaemon
userStories.description: ASRDaemon loads faster-whisper as self._secondary_model when gemini-merge post-processor is selected. Loading is non-fatal — fusion degrades gracefully to single-ASR. Secondary model unloaded when switching away from gemini-merge.
acceptanceCriteria: CRITIC-R10-L4: self._last_secondary_text = None must be initialized in __init__ alongside self._secondary_model = None
ASRDaemon._load_secondary_model() loads faster-whisper WhisperModel (CPU, large-v3-turbo, int8)
Called when post-processor switches to gemini-merge (in load_post_processor or _restore_post_processor)
self._secondary_model set to None when post-processor is not gemini-merge
Loading failure logs warning and sets self._secondary_model = None (non-fatal)
First-time model download may take time — log info message about download progress
Memory: ~1.5GB RAM for CPU model, acceptable alongside FireRedASR GPU
Tests pass

userStories.title: Create gemini-merge prompt and process function
userStories.description: New prompt file prompts/gemini-merge-system.txt instructs Gemini to merge two ASR transcriptions. New function process_with_gemini_merge() in post_processor_configs.py sends both texts to Gemini via vertex_proxy.py (same SSH mechanism as gemini-fix).
acceptanceCriteria: prompts/gemini-merge-system.txt contains merge instructions: keep Chinese from primary ASR, English terms from secondary ASR, resolve conflicts
Prompt includes: CS engineer domain context (consistent with gemini-fix-system.txt), glossary terms placeholder, examples of merge scenarios
Prompt emphasizes: secondary ASR (Whisper) is better at English technical terms; primary ASR (FireRedASR) is better at Chinese
process_with_gemini_merge(primary_text, secondary_text, config, glossary_ctx) in post_processor_configs.py
User input format: 'Chinese ASR: {primary}\nEnglish ASR: {secondary}' — vertex_proxy.py unchanged
Same guards as gemini-fix: empty text, hallucination guard (2x), timeout, SSH error handling
min_text_len guard applies to primary_text (skip merge for short text)
On failure: return primary_text (not empty string)
Prompt file deployed as copy to ~/.local/share/voice-input/prompts/ (same as other prompt files)

CRITIC-R10-M1: When secondary_text is None (fallback), gemini-merge-system.txt MUST handle single-input case explicitly — include instruction like 'If only one transcription is provided, clean it up as an editor' so the same prompt works for both merge and fallback without switching system_prompt_file
Tests pass
userStories.notes: CRITIC-R10-M1: fallback reuses same prompt+config, so prompt must be dual-purpose (merge when two inputs, polish when one input).

userStories.title: Add gemini-merge post-processor preset
userStories.description: Add 'gemini-merge' preset to POST_PROCESSOR_PRESETS in post_processor_presets.py. Framework type 'vertex-ai-merge' to distinguish from single-text 'vertex-ai'. Shares most config with gemini-fix.
acceptanceCriteria: POST_PROCESSOR_PRESETS has 'gemini-merge' entry with framework='vertex-ai-merge'
Config includes: model, vertex_region, ssh_host, timeout, min_text_len, max_text_len, system_prompt_file='gemini-merge-system.txt'
min_text_len=45 (same as gemini-fix — skip merge for short text)
PostProcessorLoader.load_post_processor() returns None for framework='vertex-ai-merge' (no local model needed)
Existing presets (none, haiku-fix, haiku-expand, gemini-fix) unchanged
CLI 'voice-input post-processor gemini-merge' switches to this preset
Tests pass

userStories.title: Integrate dual ASR fusion into ASRDaemon pipeline
userStories.description: Wire gemini-merge into ASRDaemon. _handle_transcribe() runs secondary model on same audio. _post_process() uses merge when gemini-merge is active. Fallback to primary-only if secondary fails.
acceptanceCriteria: _handle_transcribe() runs secondary model transcription when self._secondary_model is not None
Secondary transcription stored as self._last_secondary_text (or passed through return value)
Secondary model failure: log warning, set secondary_text=None, continue with primary only
_post_process() for framework='vertex-ai-merge': pipeline is filler_removal → FireRedPunc → apply_vocab → gemini_merge(primary, secondary) → diff_to_vocab → save_vocab
When secondary_text is None (model failed): fallback to gemini-fix behavior (single text polish)
Dispatch dict updated to include 'vertex-ai-merge' alongside 'ssh-claude' and 'vertex-ai'
Switching to gemini-merge triggers _load_secondary_model(); switching away unloads it
Pipeline order: primary ASR processing happens first (filler removal, punc, vocab), then merge with raw secondary text
Secondary text does NOT go through filler removal or FireRedPunc (Whisper has built-in punctuation)
Tests pass

Unit tests for process_with_gemini_merge() guards (min_text_len, max_text_len, hallucination, empty text, None secondary)
Unit tests for gemini-merge user_input format construction

userStories.title: Parallelize FireRedASR and faster-whisper transcription
userStories.description: Run FireRedASR (GPU, primary) and faster-whisper (CPU, secondary) in parallel using threading.Thread in _handle_transcribe(). FireRedASR runs in main thread (GPU), faster-whisper runs in background thread (CPU). Both release GIL via C extensions (PyTorch CUDA / CTranslate2), enabling true CPU+GPU parallelism. Reduces wall-clock latency by 0.1-2s per transcription.
acceptanceCriteria: _handle_transcribe() starts secondary ASR in a background thread BEFORE primary ASR when _secondary_model is loaded
Primary ASR runs in main thread (GPU), secondary in background thread (CPU) — no resource conflict
Both threads must complete (join) before _post_process() is called
Secondary thread uses threading.Thread(daemon=True) with join(timeout=30) to prevent hangs
Secondary result collected via mutable dict (thread-safe after join due to happens-before guarantee)
When primary fails or returns empty text: _last_secondary_text = None (secondary result discarded)
When secondary fails or times out: _last_secondary_text = None, warning logged
When no secondary model loaded: _last_secondary_text = None (fix pre-existing stale data bug)
Remove min_text_len pre-check from _handle_transcribe — downstream process_with_gemini_merge() already has identical guard at post_processor_configs.py:368
Update FUNCTION_SPEC BT#4: secondary may start in parallel, result discarded on primary failure
Update test_spec_faster_whisper.py test_primary_failure_skips_secondary to match new spec
Fix test_integration_faster_whisper.py TestDualTranscription tests (pre-existing mock target mismatch)
No new dependencies — use stdlib threading (already imported)
Existing 444 passing tests remain passing (49 pre-existing failures not increased)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Adversarial review PLAN_R1.md CONDITIONAL GO. Beads: voice_input-94f. See adversarial-review/parallelize-dual-asr/ for full plan and critique.

userStories.title: Switch primary ASR from FireRedASR to SenseVoice Small GPU
userStories.description: FireRedASR(7.2GB) + faster-whisper GPU(0.8GB) = 8.7GB > 8GB VRAM causes CUDA OOM. Switch primary ASR to SenseVoice Small(~0.5GB GPU) so both models fit in 8GB VRAM. SenseVoice has builtin punctuation, no FireRedPunc needed. Beads: voice_input-9ne.
acceptanceCriteria: DEFAULT_MODEL changed from 'firered-asr' to 'sensevoice' in model_presets.py
SenseVoice loads on GPU (no force_cpu), VRAM usage ~0.5GB
faster-whisper secondary loads on GPU (int8_float16), VRAM usage ~0.8GB
Total VRAM < 2GB when both models loaded (gemini-merge mode)
Punctuation is 'builtin' for sensevoice — FireRedPunc NOT loaded (saves VRAM)
gemini-merge description updated to mention SenseVoice instead of FireRedASR
README.md updated with sensevoice as default
No new test failures introduced (baseline: 45 pre-existing failures)
E2E voice test passes (daemon restart, transcription works)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Beads: voice_input-9ne. Code changes done, needs daemon restart + E2E verification.
